what_it_does: A design-capture document stating the 'gap pressure' law — that every operation running constrained against a declared registry is also a sensor for that registry, leaking at missing/wrong entries if residue is recorded not swallowed — and cataloging its preconditions, evidence, intentional uses, and seven build candidates (C1–C7) plus open questions and an update protocol. Nothing in it is built; it is captured as candidates for a future builder session.
observations: frontmatter: type design-capture, status 'candidates — NOT built', captured 2026-06-10, with typed relates-to wikilinks
opens with Tim's verbatim directive to capture everything as candidates with learnings for another session to pick up
naming is explicitly OPEN: gap pressure / misfit residue / the drop ledger / constrained operation is a sensor

states the law and four preconditions (closed declared vocabulary, grounded operation, fail-loud never coerce, residue recorded as data), each with the failure it prevents; removing any one kills the sensor
notes known limits: only detects what the worker model can name; weak pressure needs accumulation; variant-cluster convergence is the quality marker

three independent evidence instances: atlas_linker fan (135 drops, 115 being 6 variants of 'MCP Servers' → 9th domain), RG10 stubborn-floor entries via gc9_gap_intake.py, and SYSTEM-GAPS.md hand-ledger

extensive intentional-uses catalog (taxonomy bootstrapping, drift monitoring, starvation/inverse read, shadow-pass field sensing, cross-model triangulation, canary categories, MCP-face misuse, retrieval-side pressure, operator corrections as drops)
C1–C7 candidates with seams, verify-by-use checks, all marked unbuilt; recommended build order
adjacent candidate: evidence-in-error for parse failures in fabric/client.py, plus a stale-server-process incident note
open questions on threshold semantics, dedupe, vocabulary-mode placement, and G24 schema-decoding interaction
standouts: the reframing 'gaps are detected as pressure, not absence — you cannot query for what's missing, but a constrained system under load leaks at exactly the missing places'
the claim that gap pressure is not a feature but a PROPERTY that existing Company laws compose into, unplanned
fail-loud framed as the thing that preserves evidence: 'silent coercion eats the evidence'
each candidate is independently provable by use before the next stands on it, with a concrete instance-1 replay as the verification
conventions: follows the vault frontmatter + typed-relations + status discipline
candidates-not-built / propose-then-build epistemic discipline with explicit verify-by-use per stage
node-id naming tying into GC2 (REGISTRY-FILLING-PATTERN), G24, RG10
living-capture update protocol (flip the red marker, link the commit, update the AGENTS.md pointer)
notes: The capture of a discovered system PROPERTY (constrained ops are free gap sensors) with a 7-stage candidate build plan and three real evidence instances — a builder's pickup brief.
questions: Which candidate name will Tim choose among the four offered?
Will the vocabulary mode live on per-role output_fields or as a shared referenced registry list (the doc leans toward the latter)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it states it is a candidates capture awaiting a builder and delivers law + evidence + uses + ordered candidates + open questions, building nothing.
apparent_themes: constrained operation as a sensor (gap pressure)
registry-is-truth and fail-loud composing into emergent self-improvement
accumulation as the path to legibility
intention_signals: Trying to crystallize a discovered property of the Company's own laws into a named, evidenced, buildable plan so a future session can make gap-detection an automatic engine-native byproduct of normal constrained work, feeding the registry-growing pipeline.
novelty: The whole framing that gap detection is a free emergent byproduct of four pre-existing laws, sensed as leakage/pressure rather than queried as absence, with a rich catalog of derived sensing modes (starvation, shadow-pass, canary, cross-model triangulation).
contribution: Contributes the gap-pressure law with its four preconditions and failure-each-prevents, three evidence instances, the intentional-uses catalog, and the C1–C7 candidate build plan with seams and verify-by-use checks plus the open builder questions.
summary_for_embedding: A design-capture stating the gap-pressure law (constrained registry-bound operations are free gap sensors via recorded drops) with preconditions, three evidence instances, a uses catalog, and seven unbuilt build candidates.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and make buildable the discovery that recording the residue of constrained operations turns every normal operation into an automatic detector of missing registry entries.
